sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Clicking the blue circle didn't change anything visible. Let me check what changed by looking at the current segmentation. Maybe I need to try clicking one of the S dots instead. Let me check the current state first.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
print("Nodes:", len(nodes))
for n in nodes:
    b = n['boundary']
    cr = sum(p[0] for p in b)/len(b)
    cc = sum(p[1] for p in b)/len(b)
    print(n['id'], n['color'], n['pixels'], int(cr), int(cc))
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

The B (black background) gained 2 pixels and the W (white bar) lost 2 pixels. So the bar) is the timer. The timer shrunk by 2 pixels. The blue circle didn't change. So clicking the blue circle just advanced the timer.
Let me try clicking one of the S dots instead. Maybe I need to click them in some order. Let me try clicking the closest S dot to the blue circle. The S dots are at:
- (37,18), (37,41), (40,37), (41,16), (54,49), (55,14), (56,47), (57,16)
The blue circle is at (27,33). The closest S dot would be (37,18) or (37,41). Let me try clicking (37,18) first.
